articles.title: The Agriculture Industry Over the Years
articles.content:
As we discuss the agriculture industry, it is only appropriate for us to recognize its contributions to society, both in the economic and social aspects. Economically,A from 1998 to 2009, the agriculture sector accounted for 13.5% of the Philippines??T total gross domestic product (GDP).A At one point, its once-steady contributions dipped to 10%[1].

However, it is worth noting that this decline does not equate to a shrink in crop production. Rather, it shines a light on the different structural transformations that the agriculture sector has stood witness to. Political hurdles such as the Russian-Ukraine war, global security threats in the form of the COVID-19 pandemic, rising inflation, the decreasing number of individuals choosing to pursue careers in the agriculture sector, and natural calamities are all factors that have a direct impact on the crop and fisheries production of the country[2].A A 

But despite these challenges, the agriculture industry continues to be a primary contributor to society, and in 2023 was able to economically provide 9% of the Philippines??T GDP.A 

In the social aspect of things, theA agriculture industry was able to provide employment opportunities to around 25% of the 49.7 million Filipinos in the labor force. It has also extended support to other forms of agricultural livelihoods: fishing, raising livestock, and other non-farm activities. All of that said, the agriculture industry is a vital component of the country??Ts landscape.A
articles.author: Kabraso Multi Purpose Cooperative

news.title: NRAM
news.content:
As part of the annual National Rice Awareness Month (NRAM) celebration, the Department of Agriculture-National Rice Program (DA-NRP) organized a series of activities to promote the consumption of healthier rice alternatives and responsible rice consumption. Among these initiatives was a brown rice feeding program and the ??oBe RICEponsible??? campaign, which took place on November 13 and 19, 2024, at Project 6 Elementary School in Quezon City. 

The event aimed to educate young students about the nutritional benefits of brown rice, emphasizing its role in improving overall health and well-being. Brown rice, being less processed than white rice, retains more nutrients such as fiber, vitamins, and minerals, which are essential for growth and development. By introducing brown rice at a young age, the DA-NRP hopes to encourage healthier eating habits among children and their families.

In addition to the feeding program, the ??oBe RICEponsible??? campaign highlighted the importance of minimizing rice wastage and supporting local farmers by choosing locally-produced rice varieties. Teachers, students, and parents participated in discussions and interactive activities designed to instill a deeper appreciation for rice as a staple food and the hard work that goes into its production. 

The DA-NRP emphasized that events like these are crucial in aligning the public's eating habits with sustainable agricultural practices, contributing to the country??Ts food security goals. The celebration of NRAM, held every November, underscores the agency's commitment to fostering a culture of mindfulness and responsibility when it comes to rice consumption in the Philippines.

multimedia.title: Rice Production this Decade
multimedia.content: This planting decade showcased the remarkable efficiency and innovation of our farmers, who adopted modern techniques and sustainable practices to maximize productivity. Through their dedication and resourcefulness, they have ensured a promising harvest while minimizing environmental impact. Their efforts exemplify the future of agriculture, combining tradition with innovation to meet the needs of our growing communities.

multimedia.title: RAC Advanced Rice Production Course
multimedia.content: The Advanced Rice Production Course is a five-day training program that will cover all the necessary elements in growing and producing rice: from land preparation to post-harvest operations. Participants will also explore the best practices, innovations, and trends surrounding rice production.

multimedia.title: Aquaculture to drive up Philippine fish harvest
multimedia.content:
The country??Ts fisheries production is projected to rise by 12 percent to almost 2.9 million metric tons (MT) in 2032 on the back of higher aquaculture output, the United Nations??T Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said.
In its latest global report, the FAO estimated that Philippine fish output in 2032 would reach 2.881 million MT, up from 2.572 million MT recorded in 2022.

multimedia.title: Aquaculture Philippines 2024
multimedia.content: The Department of Agriculture (DA)??"through the Office of the Undersecretary for Livestock, the National Livestock Program (NLP), and the Bureau of Fisheries and Aquatic Resources (BFAR)??"collaborates anew with private events organizer Informa Markets in showcasing the status, trends, issues, and opportunities in the Philippine livestock, poultry, and fisheries subsectors at the Livestock and Aquaculture Philippines 2024.

multimedia.title: Philippine pork production and consumption to increase in 2025
multimedia.content: the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) released its latest data on the country??Ts hog production for the second quarter (Q2) of 2024. The data revealed a decrease in the volume of hogs produced from 422,718.73 metric tons (MT) in Q2 2023 to 422,064.15 MT in Q2 2024 primarily due to the adverse effects of El NiA?o, the tropical cyclones and the increasing ASF outbreaks.

multimedia.title: 2024 Chicken Supply and Price Trends
multimedia.content: The African Swine Fever (ASF) outbreak in the Philippines has disrupted the pork supply chain, leading to a surge in chicken prices as consumers shift to alternative protein sources. According to the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) data, the price of whole chicken in July was Php 202 per kg. However, by August 18, 2024, GMA News reported that the price of whole chickens in Metro Manila??Ts public markets increased from Php 190 per kg to Php 220 per kg within a week. The upward trend continued, with prices increasing further to Php 240 per kg as of August 24, reflecting heightened demand as consumers turned away from pork due to ASF concerns.

multimedia.title: Department of Agriculture seeks more funding for high-value crops
multimedia.content: The Department of Agriculture (DA) is seeking an eight fold increase in its budget for its high-value crops (HVC) program next year to improve the productivity of around 30 commodities and expand exports of key commodities.

multimedia.title: THE BANANA Conference and Exhibit 2024
multimedia.content: (BanCon 2024) was attended by banana farmers banana industry players, traders, and exporters and relevant stakeholders. It has an aim to improve the banana industry in the Philippines, held last Oct. 17 to 18 at SM Lanang SMX Convention Center.

Agriculture Secretary Francisco P. Tiu Laurel, Jr. said at the Pagsibol: Philippine Horticulture and Urban Agriculture Summit 2024.A

multimedia.title: Tagum hosts International Floriculture Congress 2024
multimedia.content: THE City Government of Tagum, in collaboration with the Tagum City Tourism Arts and Culture Council Inc., the Floriculture Industry of Davao Inc. (Fidi), and the Tagum City Garden Club, will host the International Floriculture Congress 2024. This event aims to promote the vibrant floriculture scene in Mindanao, particularly in the Davao Region.
